general_principles.guideline: This is not a restrictive diet; it is a nutritious, balanced plan designed to work towards a healthier you. It is flexible and adapts to any lifestyle or situation.
Good nutrition is an important part of leading a healthy lifestyle. Combined with physical activity, diet helps reach and maintain a healthy weight and reduce risk of chronic diseases.
general_principles.source: Repeated across most weekly meal plan documents

general_principles.guideline: Use only 3-4 tablespoons of oil per day, preferably cold-pressed oil.
Avoid using oil at dinner specifically (Week 5 guidance), though minor exceptions occur for tadka/tempering in specific dinner recipes.
No spice podis/powders should be used in curries, such as peanut podi or dry coconut podi.
Avoid packaged/processed foods entirely: no biscuits, cookies, chips, instant foods, bakery items, or other packaged food.
Eat foods filled with 'life' - avoid processed, packaged, or reheated/leftover foods.
general_principles.source: My Tips for you to Follow (PDF)

general_principles.guideline: Avoid bad food combinations that weaken digestion: milk with meat, fish, or eggs; eggs with bananas; fruits with milk; milk with melons; ghee with honey. (Note: meat/fish/egg references are non-applicable in this fully vegetarian dataset but preserved for completeness of the original guidance.)
general_principles.source: My Tips for you to Follow (PDF)

general_principles.guideline: Avoid curd, banana, radish, and cucumber at night/dinner.
general_principles.source: My Tips for you to Follow (PDF); reinforced by 'avoid cucumber for dinner' notes in Week 11

general_principles.guideline: Lunch should be the biggest meal of the day (digestion is strongest at midday); dinner should be light, e.g. thin broth soups and light salads. Eat like a king during the day and a pauper at night.
general_principles.source: My Tips for you to Follow (PDF)

general_principles.guideline: Avoid eating between meals; let hunger build before the next meal (no snacking).
general_principles.source: My Tips for you to Follow (PDF)

general_principles.guideline: Sip warm fluids throughout the day; avoid cold and carbonated drinks. Warm water stokes digestive fire, improves digestion, and helps prevent fat accumulation.
general_principles.source: My Tips for you to Follow (PDF)

general_principles.guideline: Sit down to eat, chew well, and avoid passive entertainment while eating (no TV, computer, cell phone, tablets, or reading).
general_principles.source: My Tips for you to Follow (PDF)

general_principles.guideline: Spice meals with fresh ginger, cumin, black pepper, turmeric, and fenugreek to enhance digestion and support weight loss goals.
general_principles.source: My Tips for you to Follow (PDF)

general_principles.guideline: Eat less, live longer - watch portions; skip second or third helpings and stop eating before reaching maximum fullness.
general_principles.source: My Tips for you to Follow (PDF)

general_principles.guideline: Respect the 24-hour solar day - time meals to align with the body's natural digestive rhythm.
general_principles.source: My Tips for you to Follow (PDF)

general_principles.guideline: Have a 1-inch piece of fresh ginger with a few drops of lemon juice and a pinch of salt, chewed before meals, to activate salivary glands and improve nutrient absorption.
Eat a small plate of salad first, before starting the main meal (both lunch and dinner).
general_principles.source: Repeated across nearly all weekly meal plans

general_principles.guideline: Sample recipe links and measurement guidance are available in the BASIC_HEALTHY_DIET document; please follow the plan as written since deviation from the plan gives no results.
Avoid potato, corn, raw banana, packed food, sugar, and honey throughout the program.
Restrict white rice and potato; can substitute any sabzi/vegetable with dal or leafy vegetables.
Avoid maida (refined flour), sweets, sugars, high-GI foods, packaged or bakery products, and potatoes.
Reduce consumption of potato, yam, and tapioca.
Avoid too much sweet food and avoid deep fried food, even on a scheduled cheat meal.
When eating out / on a cheat meal: eat per your portion size, avoid too much sweet, avoid deep fried food, prefer grilled and less oily protein, no sauces or mayo.
Avoid Maida, sweets, sugars, high-GI foods, packed/bakery products, and potatoes (repeated emphasis, Week 11 dinner section).
Tea and coffee only in the evening, with less sugar, and limited to 100ml per day combined; never on an empty stomach.
Avoid Packet Milk; use only local dairy farm milk - never use slim/low-fat packaged milk.
No cheats allowed in between the scheduled program; refunds are not possible once a meal plan has been shared.

Fast a few times a year, or at least once a season ('detox on the equinox'). Four fasting protocols from least to most vigorous: (1) light, easily digestible foods only such as khichadi, soupy broths, warm herbal teas; (2) fruits, vegetables, or fresh juices only; (3) avoiding solid foods and drinking only water or herbal teas; (a fourth, most vigorous protocol was referenced but not detailed in the source text).
general_principles.source: My Tips for you to Follow (PDF)

general_principles.guideline: Strive to align your goals, values, talents, and passions in work that supports your livelihood and gives a sense of appreciation; make room for fun and laughter, as laughter lightens the body and heightens awareness ('a belly full of laughs is a hot belly indeed').
general_principles.source: My Tips for you to Follow (PDF)

weight_loss_protocol_notes.note: Meal plans are customized week-to-week based on the individual's prior week's weight loss progress; without a weight update, no further meal plan will be issued, or the same diet may be repeated.
If there is no weight loss or weight is stuck, the meal plan will be changed accordingly.
